2100 std::ranges::end(first_range),
2101 std::ranges::begin(second_range),
2102 std::ranges::end(second_range))))
2103 : ChainAdapter(
2104 std::ranges::begin(first_range), std::ranges::end(first_range),
2105 std::ranges::begin(second_range), std::ranges::end(second_range)) {}
2106
2107 /**
2108 * @brief Constructs a chain adapter with the given const ranges.
2109 * @tparam R1 The type of the first range
2110 * @tparam R2 The type of the second range
2111 * @param first_range The first range to adapt
2112 * @param second_range The second range to adapt
2113 */
2114 template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
2115 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
2116 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>
2117 constexpr ChainAdapter(
2118 const R1& first_range,
2119 const R2&
2120 second_range) noexcept(noexcept(ChainAdapter(std::ranges::
2121 cbegin(first_range),
2122 std::ranges::cend(
2123 first_range),
2124 std::ranges::cbegin(
2125 second_range),
2126 std::ranges::cend(
2127 second_range))))
2128 : ChainAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(first_range),
2129 std::ranges::cend(first_range),
2130 std::ranges::cbegin(second_range),
2131 std::ranges::cend(second_range)) {}
2132
2133 constexpr ChainAdapter(const ChainAdapter&) noexcept(
2134 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
2135 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter2>) = default;
2136 constexpr ChainAdapter(ChainAdapter&&) noexcept(
2137 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
2138 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter2>) = default;
2139 constexpr ~ChainAdapter() noexcept(std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter1> &&
2140 std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter2>) =
2141 default;
2142
2143 constexpr ChainAdapter& operator=(const ChainAdapter&) noexcept(
2144 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter1> &&
2145 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter2>) = default;
2146 constexpr ChainAdapter& operator=(ChainAdapter&&) noexcept(
2147 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter1> &&
2148 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter2>) = default;
2149
2150 constexpr ChainAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
2151 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
2152 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter2&>()) &&
2153 noexcept(std::declval<Iter1&>() == std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
2154 noexcept(std::declval<Iter2&>() != std::declval<Iter2&>()));
2155 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ChainAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
2156 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ChainAdapter> &&
2157 noexcept(++std::declval<ChainAdapter&>()));
2158
2159 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const
2160 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<const Iter1&>()) &&
2161 noexcept(*std::declval<const Iter2&>())) {
2162 return in_first_ ? *first_current_ : *second_current_;
2163 }
2164
2165 pointer operator->() const = delete;
2166
2167 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const ChainAdapter& other) const
2168 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter1&>() ==
2169 std::declval<const Iter1&>()) &&
2170 noexcept(std::declval<const Iter2&>() ==
2171 std::declval<const Iter2&>()));
2172
2173 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator!=(const ChainAdapter& other) const
2174 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const ChainAdapter&>() ==
2175 std::declval<const ChainAdapter&>())) {
2176 return !(*this == other);
2177 }
2178
2179 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ChainAdapter begin() const
2180 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ChainAdapter>) {
2181 return *this;
2182 }
2183
2184 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ChainAdapter end() const
2185 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ChainAdapter> &&
2186 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
2187 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter2>);
2188
2189private:
2190 Iter1 first_current_; ///< Current position in first iterator
2191 Iter1 first_end_; ///< End of first iterator range
2192 Iter2 second_current_; ///< Current position in second iterator
2193 Iter2 second_end_; ///< End of second iterator range
2194 bool in_first_ = true; ///< Whether iterating through first range
2195};
2196
2197template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
2198 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R1>,
2199 std::ranges::iterator_t<R2>>
2200ChainAdapter(R1&, R2&)
2201 -> ChainAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R1>, std::ranges::iterator_t<R2>>;
2202
2203template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
2204 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
2205 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>
2206ChainAdapter(const R1&, const R2&)
2207 -> ChainAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
2208 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>;
2209
2210template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
2211 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
2212constexpr auto ChainAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::operator++() noexcept(
2213 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter1&>()) && noexcept(++std::declval<Iter2&>()) &&
2214 noexcept(std::declval<Iter1&>() == std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
2215 noexcept(std::declval<Iter2&>() != std::declval<Iter2&>()))
2216 -> ChainAdapter& {
2217 if (in_first_) {
2218 ++first_current_;
2219 if (first_current_ == first_end_) {
2220 in_first_ = false;
2221 }
2222 } else {
2223 if (second_current_ != second_end_) {
2224 ++second_current_;
2225 }
2226 }
2227 return *this;
2228}
2229
2230template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
2231 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
2232constexpr auto ChainAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::operator++(int) noexcept(
2233 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ChainAdapter> &&
2234 noexcept(++std::declval<ChainAdapter&>())) -> ChainAdapter {
2235 auto temp = *this;
2236 ++(*this);
2237 return temp;
2238}
2239
2240template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
2241 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
2242constexpr bool ChainAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::operator==(const ChainAdapter& other)
2243 const noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter1&>() ==
2244 std::declval<const Iter1&>()) &&
2245 noexcept(std::declval<const Iter2&>() ==
2246 std::declval<const Iter2&>())) {
2247 // Check if both are at end (in second range and at second_end_)
2248 const bool this_at_end = !in_first_ && (second_current_ == second_end_);
2249 const bool other_at_end =
2250 !other.in_first_ && (other.second_current_ == other.second_end_);
2251
2252 if (this_at_end && other_at_end) {
2253 return true;
2254 }
2255
2256 // Otherwise, must be in same range at same position
2257 if (in_first_ != other.in_first_) {
2258 return false;
2259 }
2260
2261 return in_first_ ? (first_current_ == other.first_current_)
2262 : (second_current_ == other.second_current_);
2263}
2264
2265template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
2266 requires ChainAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
2267constexpr auto ChainAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::end() const
2268 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ChainAdapter> &&
2269 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
2270 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter2>) -> ChainAdapter {
2271 auto end_iter = *this;
2272 end_iter.first_current_ = first_end_;
2273 end_iter.second_current_ = second_end_;
2274 end_iter.in_first_ = false;
2275 return end_iter;
2276}
2277
2278/**
2279 * @brief Adapter that iterates through elements in reverse order.
2280 * @details Requires a bidirectional iterator. Uses operator-- to traverse
2281 * backwards.
2282 * @tparam Iter Type of the underlying iterator
2283 *
2284 * @code
2285 * // Reverse iterate through entities
2286 * auto reversed = query.Reverse();
2287 * @endcode
2288 */
2289template <typename Iter>
2290 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2291class ReverseAdapter final
2292 : public FunctionalAdapterBase<ReverseAdapter<Iter>> {
2293public:
2294 using iterator_concept =
2295 std::conditional_t<details::IterYieldsReference<Iter>,
2296 std::bidirectional_iterator_tag,
2297 std::input_iterator_tag>;
2298 using iterator_category =
2299 std::conditional_t<details::IterYieldsReference<Iter>,
2300 std::bidirectional_iterator_tag,
2301 std::input_iterator_tag>;
2302 using value_type = std::iter_value_t<Iter>;
2303 using reference = value_type;
2304 using pointer = void;
2305 using difference_type = std::iter_difference_t<Iter>;
2306
2307 /**
2308 * @brief Constructs a reverse adapter.
2309 * @param begin Iterator to the beginning of the range
2310 * @param end Iterator to the end of the range
2311 */
2312 constexpr ReverseAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end) noexcept(
2313 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2314 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2315 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2316 noexcept(--std::declval<Iter&>()));
2317
2318 /**
2319 * @brief Constructs a reverse adapter with the given range.
2320 * @tparam R The type of the range
2321 * @param range The range to adapt
2322 */
2323 template <ExternalRange R>
2324 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>> &&
2325 std::ranges::bidirectional_range<R>
2326 explicit constexpr ReverseAdapter(R& range) noexcept(noexcept(
2327 ReverseAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range))))
2328 : ReverseAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range)) {}
2329
2330 /**
2331 * @brief Constructs a reverse adapter with the given const range.
2332 * @tparam R The type of the range
2333 * @param range The range to adapt
2334 */
2335 template <ExternalRange R>
2336 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>> &&
2337 std::ranges::bidirectional_range<R>
2338 explicit constexpr ReverseAdapter(const R& range) noexcept(noexcept(
2339 ReverseAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range))))
2340 : ReverseAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range)) {}
2341
2342 constexpr ReverseAdapter(const ReverseAdapter&) noexcept(
2343 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
2344 constexpr ReverseAdapter(ReverseAdapter&&) noexcept(
2345 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
2346 constexpr ~ReverseAdapter() noexcept(std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter>) =
2347 default;
2348
2349 constexpr ReverseAdapter& operator=(const ReverseAdapter&) noexcept(
2350 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
2351 constexpr ReverseAdapter& operator=(ReverseAdapter&&) noexcept(
2352 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
2353
2354 constexpr ReverseAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
2355 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2356 noexcept(--std::declval<Iter&>()));
2357 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ReverseAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
2358 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter> &&
2359 noexcept(++std::declval<ReverseAdapter&>()));
2360
2361 constexpr ReverseAdapter& operator--() noexcept(
2362 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2363 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2364 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()));
2365 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ReverseAdapter operator--(int) noexcept(
2366 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter> &&
2367 noexcept(--std::declval<ReverseAdapter&>()));
2368
2369 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const
2370 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
2371 return *current_;
2372 }
2373
2374 pointer operator->() const = delete;
2375
2376 constexpr bool operator==(const ReverseAdapter& other) const
2377 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
2378 std::declval<const Iter&>()));
2379 constexpr bool operator!=(const ReverseAdapter& other) const
2380 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<ReverseAdapter>() ==
2381 std::declval<ReverseAdapter>())) {
2382 return !(*this == other);
2383 }
2384
2385 constexpr ReverseAdapter begin() const
2386 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter>) {
2387 return *this;
2388 }
2389
2390 constexpr ReverseAdapter end() const
2391 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter>);
2392
2393private:
2394 Iter begin_;
2395 Iter current_;
2396 Iter end_;
2397 bool done_ = false;
2398};
2399
2400template <ExternalRange R>
2401ReverseAdapter(R&) -> ReverseAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>;
2402
2403template <ExternalRange R>
2404ReverseAdapter(const R&) -> ReverseAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>;
2405
2406template <typename Iter>
2407 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2408constexpr ReverseAdapter<Iter>::ReverseAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end) noexcept(
2409 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2410 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2411 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2412 noexcept(--std::declval<Iter&>()))
2413 : begin_(std::move(begin)),
2414 current_(std::move(end)),
2415 end_(current_),
2416 done_(begin_ == end_) {
2417 if (current_ != begin_) {
2418 --current_;
2419 }
2420}
2421
2422template <typename Iter>
2423 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2424constexpr auto ReverseAdapter<Iter>::operator++() noexcept(
2425 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2426 noexcept(--std::declval<Iter&>())) -> ReverseAdapter& {
2427 if (!done_) {
2428 if (current_ == begin_) {
2429 done_ = true;
2430 } else {
2431 --current_;
2432 }
2433 }
2434 return *this;
2435}
2436
2437template <typename Iter>
2438 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2439constexpr auto ReverseAdapter<Iter>::operator++(int) noexcept(
2440 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter> &&
2441 noexcept(++std::declval<ReverseAdapter&>())) -> ReverseAdapter {
2442 auto temp = *this;
2443 ++(*this);
2444 return temp;
2445}
2446
2447template <typename Iter>
2448 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2449constexpr auto ReverseAdapter<Iter>::operator--() noexcept(
2450 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2451 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2452 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()))
2453 -> ReverseAdapter& {
2454 if (done_) {
2455 done_ = false;
2456 current_ = begin_;
2457 } else {
2458 if (current_ != end_) {
2459 ++current_;
2460 }
2461 }
2462 return *this;
2463}
2464
2465template <typename Iter>
2466 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2467constexpr auto ReverseAdapter<Iter>::operator--(int) noexcept(
2468 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter> &&
2469 noexcept(--std::declval<ReverseAdapter&>())) -> ReverseAdapter {
2470 auto temp = *this;
2471 --(*this);
2472 return temp;
2473}
2474
2475template <typename Iter>
2476 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2477constexpr bool ReverseAdapter<Iter>::operator==(const ReverseAdapter& other)
2478 const noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
2479 std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
2480 if (done_ && other.done_) {
2481 return true;
2482 }
2483 return done_ == other.done_ && current_ == other.current_;
2484}
2485
2486template <typename Iter>
2487 requires ReverseAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2488constexpr auto ReverseAdapter<Iter>::end() const
2489 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ReverseAdapter>)
2490 -> ReverseAdapter {
2491 auto result = *this;
2492 result.done_ = true;
2493 return result;
2494}
2495
2496/**
2497 * @brief Adapter that flattens nested ranges into a single sequence.
2498 * @details Iterates through an outer range of ranges, yielding elements from
2499 * inner ranges.
2500 * @tparam Iter Type of the outer iterator
2501 *
2502 * @code
2503 * // Join queries of `Position` and `Velocity` components
2504 * auto joined = query1.Join(query2);
2505 * joined.ForEach([](const Position& pos, const Velocity& vel) {
2506 */
2507template <typename Iter>
2508 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2509class JoinAdapter final : public FunctionalAdapterBase<JoinAdapter<Iter>> {
2510public:
2511 using iterator_concept = details::adapter_iterator_concept_t<Iter>;
2512 using iterator_category = details::adapter_iterator_category_t<Iter>;
2513 using outer_value_type = std::iter_value_t<Iter>;
2514 using inner_iterator_type = std::ranges::iterator_t<outer_value_type>;
2515 using value_type = std::iter_value_t<inner_iterator_type>;
2516 using difference_type = ptrdiff_t;
2517 using pointer = value_type*;
2518 using reference = value_type;
2519
2520 /**
2521 * @brief Constructs a join adapter.
2522 * @param begin Iterator to the beginning of the outer range
2523 * @param end Iterator to the end of the outer range
2524 */
2525 constexpr JoinAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end) noexcept(
2526 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2527 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2528 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<inner_iterator_type> &&
2529 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2530 noexcept(*std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2531 noexcept(AdvanceToValid()));
2532
2533 /**
2534 * @brief Constructs a join adapter from a range.
2535 * @tparam R The type of the range
2536 * @param range The range to adapt
2537 */
2538 template <ExternalRange R>
2539 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
2540 explicit constexpr JoinAdapter(R& range) noexcept(
2541 noexcept(JoinAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range))))
2542 : JoinAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range)) {}
2543
2544 /**
2545 * @brief Constructs a join adapter from a range.
2546 * @tparam R The type of the range
2547 * @param range The range to adapt
2548 */
2549 template <ExternalRange R>
2550 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
2551 explicit constexpr JoinAdapter(const R& range) noexcept(noexcept(
2552 JoinAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range))))
2553 : JoinAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range)) {}
2554
2555 constexpr JoinAdapter(const JoinAdapter&) noexcept(
2556 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2557 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<inner_iterator_type>) = default;
2558
2559 constexpr JoinAdapter(JoinAdapter&&) noexcept(
2560 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2561 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<inner_iterator_type>) = default;
2562 constexpr ~JoinAdapter() noexcept(
2563 std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter> &&
2564 std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<inner_iterator_type>) = default;
2565
2566 constexpr JoinAdapter& operator=(const JoinAdapter&) noexcept(
2567 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter> &&
2568 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<inner_iterator_type>) = default;
2569 constexpr JoinAdapter& operator=(JoinAdapter&&) noexcept(
2570 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter> &&
2571 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<inner_iterator_type>) = default;
2572
2573 constexpr JoinAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
2574 noexcept(std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>() !=
2575 std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2576 noexcept(++std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2577 noexcept(AdvanceToValid()));
2578 [[nodiscard]] constexpr JoinAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
2579 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<JoinAdapter> &&
2580 noexcept(++std::declval<JoinAdapter&>()));
2581
2582 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const
2583 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<const inner_iterator_type&>())) {
2584 return *inner_current_;
2585 }
2586
2587 pointer operator->() const = delete;
2588
2589 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const JoinAdapter& other) const
2590 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
2591 std::declval<const Iter&>()) &&
2592 noexcept(std::declval<const inner_iterator_type&>() ==
2593 std::declval<const inner_iterator_type&>()));
2594 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator!=(const JoinAdapter& other) const
2595 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<JoinAdapter>() ==
2596 std::declval<JoinAdapter>())) {
2597 return !(*this == other);
2598 }
2599
2600 [[nodiscard]] constexpr JoinAdapter begin() const noexcept(
2601 std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<JoinAdapter, const Iter&, const Iter&>) {
2602 return {outer_begin_, outer_end_};
2603 }
2604
2605 [[nodiscard]] constexpr JoinAdapter end() const
2606 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<JoinAdapter> &&
2607 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>);
2608
2609private:
2610 constexpr void AdvanceToValid() noexcept(
2611 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2612 noexcept(std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>() ==
2613 std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2614 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()) && noexcept(*std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2615 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<inner_iterator_type>);
2616
2617 Iter outer_begin_;
2618 Iter outer_current_;
2619 Iter outer_end_;
2620 inner_iterator_type inner_current_;
2621 inner_iterator_type inner_end_;
2622};
2623
2624template <ExternalRange R>
2625 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
2626JoinAdapter(R&) -> JoinAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>;
2627
2628template <ExternalRange R>
2629 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
2630JoinAdapter(const R&) -> JoinAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>;
2631
2632template <typename Iter>
2633 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2634constexpr JoinAdapter<Iter>::JoinAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end) noexcept(
2635 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2636 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2637 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<inner_iterator_type> &&
2638 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2639 noexcept(*std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2640 noexcept(AdvanceToValid()))
2641 : outer_begin_(std::move(begin)),
2642 outer_current_(outer_begin_),
2643 outer_end_(std::move(end)) {
2644 if (outer_current_ != outer_end_) {
2645 auto& inner_range = *outer_current_;
2646 inner_current_ = std::ranges::begin(inner_range);
2647 inner_end_ = std::ranges::end(inner_range);
2648 }
2649 AdvanceToValid();
2650}
2651
2652template <typename Iter>
2653 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2654constexpr auto JoinAdapter<Iter>::operator++() noexcept(
2655 noexcept(std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>() !=
2656 std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2657 noexcept(++std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2658 noexcept(AdvanceToValid())) -> JoinAdapter& {
2659 if (inner_current_ != inner_end_) {
2660 ++inner_current_;
2661 }
2662 AdvanceToValid();
2663 return *this;
2664}
2665
2666template <typename Iter>
2667 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2668constexpr auto JoinAdapter<Iter>::operator++(int) noexcept(
2669 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<JoinAdapter> &&
2670 noexcept(++std::declval<JoinAdapter&>())) -> JoinAdapter {
2671 auto temp = *this;
2672 ++(*this);
2673 return temp;
2674}
2675
2676template <typename Iter>
2677 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2678constexpr bool JoinAdapter<Iter>::operator==(const JoinAdapter& other) const
2679 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
2680 std::declval<const Iter&>()) &&
2681 noexcept(std::declval<const inner_iterator_type&>() ==
2682 std::declval<const inner_iterator_type&>())) {
2683 if (outer_current_ != other.outer_current_) {
2684 return false;
2685 }
2686 if (outer_current_ == outer_end_) {
2687 return true;
2688 }
2689 return inner_current_ == other.inner_current_;
2690}
2691
2692template <typename Iter>
2693 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2694constexpr auto JoinAdapter<Iter>::end() const
2695 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<JoinAdapter> &&
2696 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) -> JoinAdapter {
2697 auto result = *this;
2698 result.outer_current_ = result.outer_end_;
2699 return result;
2700}
2701
2702template <typename Iter>
2703 requires JoinAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2704constexpr void JoinAdapter<Iter>::AdvanceToValid() noexcept(
2705 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() == std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2706 noexcept(std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>() ==
2707 std::declval<inner_iterator_type&>()) &&
2708 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()) && noexcept(*std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
2709 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<inner_iterator_type>) {
2710 while (outer_current_ != outer_end_) {
2711 if (inner_current_ == inner_end_) {
2712 ++outer_current_;
2713 if (outer_current_ == outer_end_) {
2714 return;
2715 }
2716 auto& inner_range = *outer_current_;
2717 inner_current_ = std::ranges::begin(inner_range);
2718 inner_end_ = std::ranges::end(inner_range);
2719 }
2720
2721 if (inner_current_ != inner_end_) {
2722 return;
2723 }
2724 }
2725}
2726
2727/**
2728 * @brief A non-owning view over a sliding window of elements.
2729 * @details Provides a lightweight, non-allocating view over a contiguous window
2730 * of elements.
2731 * @tparam Iter Type of the underlying iterator
2732 *
2733 * @code
2734 * std::vector<int> data = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};
2735 * auto slide = SlideAdapterFromRange(data, 3);
2736 * for (const auto& window : slide) {
2737 * // window is a SlideView, iterate over it
2738 * for (int val : window) {
2739 * std::cout << val << " ";
2740 * }
2741 * std::cout << "\n";
2742 * }
2743 * // Output:
2744 * // 1 2 3
2745 * // 2 3 4
2746 * // 3 4 5
2747 * @endcode
2748 */
2749template <IteratorLike Iter>
2750class SlideView {
2751public:
2752 using iterator = Iter;
2753 using const_iterator = Iter;
2754 using value_type = std::iter_value_t<Iter>;
2755 using reference = decltype(*std::declval<Iter>());
2756 using size_type = size_t;
2757
2758 /**
2759 * @brief Constructs a SlideView.
2760 * @param begin Iterator to the beginning of the window
2761 * @param size Size of the window
2762 */
2763 constexpr SlideView(Iter begin, size_t size) noexcept(
2764 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>)
2765 : begin_(begin), size_(size) {}
2766
2767 constexpr SlideView(const SlideView&) noexcept(
2768 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
2769 constexpr SlideView(SlideView&&) noexcept(
2770 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
2771 constexpr ~SlideView() noexcept = default;
2772
2773 constexpr SlideView& operator=(const SlideView&) noexcept(
2774 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
2775 constexpr SlideView& operator=(SlideView&&) noexcept(
2776 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
2777
2778 /**
2779 * @brief Collects the window elements into a vector.
2780 * @details Use when you need ownership of the elements.
2781 * @return Vector containing copies of the window elements
2782 */
2783 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Collect() const -> std::vector<value_type>
2784 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>;
2785
2786 /**
2787 * @brief Collects the window elements into a vector with a custom allocator.
2788 * @details Use when you need ownership of the elements with a specific
2789 * allocator.
2790 * @tparam Allocator Allocator type
2791 * @param allocator Allocator to use for the vector
2792 * @return Vector containing copies of the window elements
2793 */
2794 template <typename Allocator>
2795 requires(!std::derived_from<std::remove_pointer_t<Allocator>,
2796 std::pmr::memory_resource>)
2797 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto CollectWith(Allocator allocator = {}) const
2798 -> std::vector<value_type, Allocator>
2799 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>;
2800
2801 /**
2802 * @brief Collects the window elements into a vector with a specific memory
2803 * resource.
2804 * @details Use when you need ownership of the elements with a specific
2805 * memory resource.
2806 * @param resource Memory resource to use for the vector
2807 * @return Vector containing copies of the window elements
2808 */
2809 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto CollectWith(
2810 std::pmr::memory_resource* resource) const -> std::pmr::vector<value_type>
2811 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>;
2812
2813 auto CollectWith(std::nullptr_t) const
2814 -> std::pmr::vector<value_type> = delete;
2815
2816 /**
2817 * @brief Accesses an element by index.
2818 * @warning Index must be less than size
2819 * @param index Index of the element
2820 * @return Reference to the element
2821 */
2822 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator[](size_t index) const
2823 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<Iter>()) &&
2824 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
2825
2826 /**
2827 * @brief Compares two SlideViews for equality.
2828 * @param other SlideView to compare with
2829 * @return True if both views have the same elements
2830 */
2831 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const SlideView& other) const;
2832
2833 /**
2834 * @brief Compares SlideView with a vector for equality.
2835 * @tparam R
2836 * @param vec Vector to compare with
2837 * @return True if the view has the same elements as the vector
2838 */
2839 template <std::ranges::sized_range R>
2840 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const R& range) const;
2841
2842 /**
2843 * @brief Checks if the window is empty.
2844 * @return True if the window has no elements
2845 */
2846 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool Empty() const noexcept { return size_ == 0; }
2847
2848 /**
2849 * @brief Returns the size of the window.
2850 * @return Number of elements in the window
2851 */
2852 [[nodiscard]] constexpr size_t Size() const noexcept { return size_; }
2853
2854 /**
2855 * @brief Returns an iterator to the beginning.
2856 * @return Iterator to the first element
2857 */
2858 [[nodiscard]] constexpr Iter begin() const
2859 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>) {
2860 return begin_;
2861 }
2862
2863 /**
2864 * @brief Returns an iterator to the end.
2865 * @return Iterator past the last element
2866 */
2867 [[nodiscard]] constexpr Iter end() const
2868 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2869 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
2870
2871private:
2872 Iter begin_;
2873 size_t size_ = 0;
2874};
2875
2876template <IteratorLike Iter>
2877constexpr auto SlideView<Iter>::Collect() const -> std::vector<value_type>
2878 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>
2879{
2880 std::vector<value_type> result;
2881 result.reserve(size_);
2882 auto it = begin_;
2883 for (size_t i = 0; i < size_; ++i) {
2884 result.push_back(*it++);
2885 }
2886 return result;
2887}
2888
2889template <IteratorLike Iter>
2890template <typename Allocator>
2891 requires(!std::derived_from<std::remove_pointer_t<Allocator>,
2892 std::pmr::memory_resource>)
2893constexpr auto SlideView<Iter>::CollectWith(Allocator allocator) const
2894 -> std::vector<value_type, Allocator>
2895 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>
2896{
2897 std::vector<value_type, Allocator> result{std::move(allocator)};
2898 result.reserve(size_);
2899 auto it = begin_;
2900 for (size_t i = 0; i < size_; ++i) {
2901 result.push_back(*it++);
2902 }
2903 return result;
2904}
2905
2906template <IteratorLike Iter>
2907constexpr auto SlideView<Iter>::CollectWith(
2908 std::pmr::memory_resource* resource) const -> std::pmr::vector<value_type>
2909 requires std::copy_constructible<value_type>
2910{
2911 std::pmr::vector<value_type> result{resource};
2912 result.reserve(size_);
2913 auto it = begin_;
2914 for (size_t i = 0; i < size_; ++i) {
2915 result.push_back(*it++);
2916 }
2917 return result;
2918}
2919
2920template <IteratorLike Iter>
2921constexpr auto SlideView<Iter>::operator[](size_t index) const
2922 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<Iter>()) &&
2923 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>())) -> reference {
2924 auto it = begin_;
2925 for (size_t i = 0; i < index; ++i) {
2926 ++it;
2927 }
2928 return *it;
2929}
2930
2931template <IteratorLike Iter>
2932constexpr bool SlideView<Iter>::operator==(const SlideView& other) const {
2933 if (size_ != other.size_) {
2934 return false;
2935 }
2936
2937 auto it1 = begin_;
2938 auto it2 = other.begin_;
2939 for (size_t i = 0; i < size_; ++i) {
2940 if (*it1++ != *it2++) {
2941 return false;
2942 }
2943 }
2944 return true;
2945}
2946
2947template <IteratorLike Iter>
2948template <std::ranges::sized_range R>
2949constexpr bool SlideView<Iter>::operator==(const R& range) const {
2950 if (size_ != std::ranges::size(range)) {
2951 return false;
2952 }
2953
2954 return std::equal(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range),
2955 begin_);
2956}
2957
2958template <IteratorLike Iter>
2959constexpr Iter SlideView<Iter>::end() const
2960 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
2961 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>())) {
2962 auto it = begin_;
2963 for (size_t i = 0; i < size_; ++i) {
2964 ++it;
2965 }
2966 return it;
2967}
2968
2969/**
2970 * @brief Adapter that yields sliding windows of elements.
2971 * @details Creates overlapping windows of a fixed size, moving one element at a
2972 * time. Each window is returned as a SlideView, which is a non-allocating view
2973 * over the elements.
2974 * @tparam Iter Type of the underlying iterator
2975 *
2976 * @code
2977 * std::vector<int> data = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5};
2978 * auto slide = SlideAdapterFromRange(data, 3);
2979 *
2980 * // Iterate over windows
2981 * for (const auto& window : slide) {
2982 * // Each window is a SlideView
2983 * for (int val : window) {
2984 * std::cout << val << " ";
2985 * }
2986 * std::cout << "\n";
2987 * }
2988 *
2989 * // Collect windows if needed
2990 * auto windows = slide.Collect(); // Vector of SlideViews
2991 *
2992 * // Convert a window to vector if ownership needed
2993 * auto first_window = (*slide.begin()).Collect(); // std::vector<int>
2994 * @endcode
2995 */
2996template <typename Iter>
2997 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
2998class SlideAdapter final : public FunctionalAdapterBase<SlideAdapter<Iter>> {
2999public:
3000 using iterator_concept = std::forward_iterator_tag;
3001 using iterator_category = std::input_iterator_tag;
3002 using value_type = SlideView<Iter>;
3003 using reference = value_type;
3004 using pointer = void;
3005 using difference_type = std::iter_difference_t<Iter>;
3006
3007 /**
3008 * @brief Constructs a slide adapter.
3009 * @warning window_size must be greater than 0
3010 * @param begin Iterator to the beginning of the range
3011 * @param end Iterator to the end of the range
3012 * @param window_size Size of the sliding window
3013 */
3014 constexpr SlideAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end, size_t window_size) noexcept(
3015 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
3016 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
3017 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3018 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
3019
3020 /**
3021 * @brief Constructs a slide adapter from a range.
3022 * @tparam R The type of the range
3023 * @param range The range to adapt
3024 * @param window_size The size of the sliding window
3025 */
3026 template <ExternalRange R>
3027 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
3028 explicit constexpr SlideAdapter(R& range, size_t window_size) noexcept(
3029 noexcept(SlideAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range),
3030 window_size)))
3031 : SlideAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range),
3032 window_size) {}
3033
3034 /**
3035 * @brief Constructs a slide adapter from a const range.
3036 * @tparam R The type of the range
3037 * @param range The range to adapt
3038 * @param window_size The size of the sliding window
3039 */
3040 template <ExternalRange R>
3041 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
3042 explicit constexpr SlideAdapter(const R& range, size_t window_size) noexcept(
3043 noexcept(SlideAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range),
3044 std::ranges::cend(range), window_size)))
3045 : SlideAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range),
3046 window_size) {}
3047
3048 constexpr SlideAdapter(const SlideAdapter&) noexcept(
3049 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
3050 constexpr SlideAdapter(SlideAdapter&&) noexcept(
3051 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
3052 constexpr ~SlideAdapter() noexcept(std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter>) =
3053 default;
3054
3055 constexpr SlideAdapter& operator=(const SlideAdapter&) noexcept(
3056 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
3057 constexpr SlideAdapter& operator=(SlideAdapter&&) noexcept(
3058 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
3059
3060 constexpr SlideAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
3061 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3062 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
3063 [[nodiscard]] constexpr SlideAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
3064 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<SlideAdapter> &&
3065 noexcept(++std::declval<SlideAdapter&>()));
3066
3067 /**
3068 * @brief Dereferences the iterator to get the current window.
3069 * @return SlideView representing the current window
3070 */
3071 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const
3072 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<SlideView<Iter>, Iter, size_t>) {
3073 return {current_, window_size_};
3074 }
3075
3076 pointer operator->() const = delete;
3077
3078 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const SlideAdapter& other) const
3079 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
3080 std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
3081 return current_ == other.current_;
3082 }
3083
3084 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator!=(const SlideAdapter& other) const
3085 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() !=
3086 std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
3087 return current_ != other.current_;
3088 }
3089
3090 [[nodiscard]] constexpr SlideAdapter begin() const
3091 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<SlideAdapter, const Iter&,
3092 const Iter&, size_t>) {
3093 return {begin_, end_, window_size_};
3094 }
3095
3096 [[nodiscard]] constexpr SlideAdapter end() const
3097 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<SlideAdapter> &&
3098 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter> &&
3099 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<const Iter&>()) &&
3100 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
3101
3102 /**
3103 * @brief Returns the window size.
3104 * @return Size of the sliding window
3105 */
3106 [[nodiscard]] constexpr size_t WindowSize() const noexcept {
3107 return window_size_;
3108 }
3109
3110private:
3111 Iter begin_;
3112 Iter current_;
3113 Iter end_;
3114 size_t window_size_ = 0;
3115};
3116
3117template <ExternalRange R>
3118 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
3119SlideAdapter(R&, size_t) -> SlideAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>;
3120
3121template <ExternalRange R>
3122 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
3123SlideAdapter(const R&, size_t)
3124 -> SlideAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>;
3125
3126template <typename Iter>
3127 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3128constexpr SlideAdapter<Iter>::SlideAdapter(
3129 Iter begin, Iter end,
3130 size_t window_size) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
3131 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter> &&
3132 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() !=
3133 std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3134 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()))
3135 : begin_(std::move(begin)),
3136 current_(begin_),
3137 end_(std::move(end)),
3138 window_size_(window_size) {
3139 // If we can't form a complete window, start at end
3140 Iter iter = begin_;
3141 size_t count = 0;
3142 while (iter != end_) {
3143 ++iter;
3144 ++count;
3145 }
3146 if (count < window_size_) {
3147 current_ = end_;
3148 }
3149}
3150
3151template <typename Iter>
3152 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3153constexpr auto SlideAdapter<Iter>::operator++() noexcept(
3154 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3155 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>())) -> SlideAdapter& {
3156 if (current_ != end_) {
3157 ++current_;
3158 }
3159 return *this;
3160}
3161
3162template <typename Iter>
3163 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3164constexpr auto SlideAdapter<Iter>::operator++(int) noexcept(
3165 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<SlideAdapter> &&
3166 noexcept(++std::declval<SlideAdapter&>())) -> SlideAdapter {
3167 auto temp = *this;
3168 ++(*this);
3169 return temp;
3170}
3171
3172template <typename Iter>
3173 requires SlideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3174constexpr auto SlideAdapter<Iter>::end() const
3175 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<SlideAdapter> &&
3176 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter> &&
3177 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<const Iter&>()) &&
3178 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>())) -> SlideAdapter {
3179 auto result = *this;
3180
3181 // Calculate end position: when we can't form a complete window
3182 Iter iter = begin_;
3183 size_t count = 0;
3184 while (iter != end_) {
3185 ++iter;
3186 ++count;
3187 }
3188
3189 // Position where we can't form more windows
3190 if (count >= window_size_) {
3191 result.current_ = begin_;
3192 for (size_t i = 0; i < count - window_size_ + 1; ++i) {
3193 ++result.current_;
3194 }
3195 } else {
3196 result.current_ = end_;
3197 }
3198
3199 return result;
3200}
3201
3202/**
3203 * @brief Adapter that yields every Nth element from the range.
3204 * @details Similar to StepBy but with different semantics - takes stride, not
3205 * step.
3206 * @tparam Iter Type of the underlying iterator
3207 *
3208 * @code
3209 * std::vector<int> data = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9};
3210 * auto strided = StrideAdapterFromRange(data, 3);
3211 * // Yields: 1, 4, 7
3212 * for (int val : strided) {
3213 * std::cout << val << " ";
3214 * }
3215 * @endcode
3216 */
3217template <typename Iter>
3218 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3219class StrideAdapter final : public FunctionalAdapterBase<StrideAdapter<Iter>> {
3220public:
3221 using iterator_concept = details::adapter_iterator_concept_t<Iter>;
3222 using iterator_category = details::adapter_iterator_category_t<Iter>;
3223 using value_type = std::iter_value_t<Iter>;
3224 using reference = value_type;
3225 using pointer = void;
3226 using difference_type = std::iter_difference_t<Iter>;
3227
3228 /**
3229 * @brief Constructs a stride adapter.
3230 * @param begin Iterator to the beginning of the range
3231 * @param end Iterator to the end of the range
3232 * @param stride Number of elements to skip between yields (1 = every element,
3233 * 2 = every other, etc.)
3234 * @warning stride must be greater than 0
3235 */
3236 constexpr StrideAdapter(Iter begin, Iter end, size_t stride) noexcept(
3237 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter> &&
3238 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>)
3239 : begin_(std::move(begin)),
3240 current_(begin_),
3241 end_(std::move(end)),
3242 stride_(stride) {}
3243
3244 /**
3245 * @brief Constructs a stride adapter from a range and a stride.
3246 * @tparam R The type of the range
3247 * @param range The range to adapt
3248 * @param stride Number of elements to skip between yields (1 = every element,
3249 * 2 = every other, etc.)
3250 * @warning stride must be greater than 0
3251 */
3252 template <ExternalRange R>
3253 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
3254 constexpr StrideAdapter(R& range, size_t stride) noexcept(
3255 noexcept(StrideAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range),
3256 stride)))
3257 : StrideAdapter(std::ranges::begin(range), std::ranges::end(range),
3258 stride) {}
3259
3260 /**
3261 * @brief Constructs a stride adapter from a const range and a stride.
3262 * @tparam R The type of the range
3263 * @param range The range to adapt
3264 * @param stride Number of elements to skip between yields (1 = every element,
3265 * 2 = every other, etc.)
3266 * @warning stride must be greater than 0
3267 */
3268 template <ExternalRange R>
3269 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
3270 constexpr StrideAdapter(const R& range, size_t stride) noexcept(
3271 noexcept(StrideAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range),
3272 std::ranges::cend(range), stride)))
3273 : StrideAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(range), std::ranges::cend(range),
3274 stride) {}
3275
3276 constexpr StrideAdapter(const StrideAdapter&) noexcept(
3277 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
3278 constexpr StrideAdapter(StrideAdapter&&) noexcept(
3279 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter>) = default;
3280 constexpr ~StrideAdapter() noexcept(std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter>) =
3281 default;
3282
3283 constexpr StrideAdapter& operator=(const StrideAdapter&) noexcept(
3284 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
3285 constexpr StrideAdapter& operator=(StrideAdapter&&) noexcept(
3286 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter>) = default;
3287
3288 constexpr StrideAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
3289 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3290 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>()));
3291 [[nodiscard]] constexpr StrideAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
3292 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<StrideAdapter> &&
3293 noexcept(++std::declval<StrideAdapter&>()));
3294
3295 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const
3296 noexcept(noexcept(*std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
3297 return *current_;
3298 }
3299
3300 pointer operator->() const = delete;
3301
3302 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const StrideAdapter& other) const
3303 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() ==
3304 std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
3305 return current_ == other.current_;
3306 }
3307
3308 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator!=(const StrideAdapter& other) const
3309 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter&>() !=
3310 std::declval<const Iter&>())) {
3311 return current_ != other.current_;
3312 }
3313
3314 [[nodiscard]] constexpr StrideAdapter begin() const
3315 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<StrideAdapter, const Iter&,
3316 const Iter&, size_t>) {
3317 return {begin_, end_, stride_};
3318 }
3319
3320 [[nodiscard]] constexpr StrideAdapter end() const
3321 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<StrideAdapter> &&
3322 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>);
3323
3324private:
3325 Iter begin_;
3326 Iter current_;
3327 Iter end_;
3328 size_t stride_ = 0;
3329};
3330
3331template <ExternalRange R>
3332 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>
3333StrideAdapter(R&, size_t) -> StrideAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>;
3334
3335template <ExternalRange R>
3336 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>
3337StrideAdapter(const R&, size_t)
3338 -> StrideAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>;
3339
3340template <typename Iter>
3341 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3342constexpr auto StrideAdapter<Iter>::operator++() noexcept(
3343 noexcept(std::declval<Iter&>() != std::declval<Iter&>()) &&
3344 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter&>())) -> StrideAdapter& {
3345 for (size_t i = 0; i < stride_ && current_ != end_; ++i) {
3346 ++current_;
3347 }
3348 return *this;
3349}
3350
3351template <typename Iter>
3352 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3353constexpr auto StrideAdapter<Iter>::operator++(int) noexcept(
3354 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<StrideAdapter> &&
3355 noexcept(++std::declval<StrideAdapter&>())) -> StrideAdapter {
3356 auto temp = *this;
3357 ++(*this);
3358 return temp;
3359}
3360
3361template <typename Iter>
3362 requires StrideAdapterRequirements<Iter>
3363constexpr auto StrideAdapter<Iter>::end() const
3364 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<StrideAdapter> &&
3365 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter>) -> StrideAdapter {
3366 auto result = *this;
3367 result.current_ = end_;
3368 return result;
3369}
3370
3371/**
3372 * @brief Adapter that combines two ranges into pairs.
3373 * @details Iterates both ranges in parallel, yielding tuples of corresponding
3374 * elements. Stops when either range is exhausted.
3375 * @tparam Iter1 Type of the first iterator
3376 * @tparam Iter2 Type of the second iterator
3377 *
3378 * @code
3379 * std::vector<int> ids = {1, 2, 3};
3380 * std::vector<std::string> names = {"Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"};
3381 * auto zipped = ZipAdapterFromRange(ids, names);
3382 * for (const auto& [id, name] : zipped) {
3383 * std::cout << id << ": " << name << "\n";
3384 * }
3385 * // Output:
3386 * // 1: Alice
3387 * // 2: Bob
3388 * // 3: Charlie
3389 * @endcode
3390 */
3391template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
3392 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
3393class ZipAdapter final
3394 : public FunctionalAdapterBase<ZipAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>> {
3395public:
3396 using iterator_concept = std::input_iterator_tag;
3397 using iterator_category = std::input_iterator_tag;
3398 using value_type =
3399 std::tuple<std::iter_value_t<Iter1>, std::iter_value_t<Iter2>>;
3400 using reference = value_type;
3401 using pointer = void;
3402 using difference_type = ptrdiff_t;
3403
3404 /**
3405 * @brief Constructs a zip adapter.
3406 * @param begin1 Iterator to the beginning of the first range
3407 * @param end1 Iterator to the end of the first range
3408 * @param begin2 Iterator to the beginning of the second range
3409 * @param end2 Iterator to the end of the second range
3410 */
3411 constexpr ZipAdapter(
3412 Iter1 begin1, Iter1 end1, Iter2 begin2,
3413 Iter2 end2) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
3414 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
3415 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter2> &&
3416 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter2>)
3417 : first_begin_(std::move(begin1)),
3418 first_current_(first_begin_),
3419 first_end_(std::move(end1)),
3420 second_begin_(std::move(begin2)),
3421 second_current_(second_begin_),
3422 second_end_(std::move(end2)) {}
3423
3424 template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
3425 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R1>,
3426 std::ranges::iterator_t<R2>>
3427 constexpr ZipAdapter(R1& first_range, R2& second_range) noexcept(noexcept(
3428 ZipAdapter(std::ranges::begin(first_range), std::ranges::end(first_range),
3429 std::ranges::begin(second_range),
3430 std::ranges::end(second_range))))
3431 : ZipAdapter(
3432 std::ranges::begin(first_range), std::ranges::end(first_range),
3433 std::ranges::begin(second_range), std::ranges::end(second_range)) {}
3434
3435 template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
3436 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
3437 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>
3438 constexpr ZipAdapter(const R1& first_range, const R2& second_range) noexcept(
3439 noexcept(ZipAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(first_range),
3440 std::ranges::cend(first_range),
3441 std::ranges::cbegin(second_range),
3442 std::ranges::cend(second_range))))
3443 : ZipAdapter(std::ranges::cbegin(first_range),
3444 std::ranges::cend(first_range),
3445 std::ranges::cbegin(second_range),
3446 std::ranges::cend(second_range)) {}
3447
3448 constexpr ZipAdapter(const ZipAdapter&) noexcept(
3449 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
3450 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<Iter2>) = default;
3451 constexpr ZipAdapter(ZipAdapter&&) noexcept(
3452 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter1> &&
3453 std::is_nothrow_move_constructible_v<Iter2>) = default;
3454 constexpr ~ZipAdapter() noexcept(std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter1> &&
3455 std::is_nothrow_destructible_v<Iter2>) =
3456 default;
3457
3458 constexpr ZipAdapter& operator=(const ZipAdapter&) noexcept(
3459 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter1> &&
3460 std::is_nothrow_copy_assignable_v<Iter2>) = default;
3461 constexpr ZipAdapter& operator=(ZipAdapter&&) noexcept(
3462 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter1> &&
3463 std::is_nothrow_move_assignable_v<Iter2>) = default;
3464
3465 constexpr ZipAdapter& operator++() noexcept(
3466 noexcept(std::declval<Iter1&>() != std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
3467 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
3468 noexcept(std::declval<Iter2&>() != std::declval<Iter2&>()) &&
3469 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter2&>()));
3470
3471 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ZipAdapter operator++(int) noexcept(
3472 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ZipAdapter> &&
3473 noexcept(++std::declval<ZipAdapter&>()));
3474
3475 [[nodiscard]] constexpr reference operator*() const {
3476 return std::make_tuple(*first_current_, *second_current_);
3477 }
3478
3479 pointer operator->() const = delete;
3480
3481 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator==(const ZipAdapter& other) const
3482 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const Iter1&>() ==
3483 std::declval<const Iter1&>()) &&
3484 noexcept(std::declval<const Iter2&>() ==
3485 std::declval<const Iter2&>())) {
3486 return first_current_ == other.first_current_ ||
3487 second_current_ == other.second_current_;
3488 }
3489
3490 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool operator!=(const ZipAdapter& other) const
3491 noexcept(noexcept(std::declval<const ZipAdapter&>() ==
3492 std::declval<const ZipAdapter&>())) {
3493 return !(*this == other);
3494 }
3495
3496 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool IsAtEnd() const noexcept(
3497 noexcept(std::declval<const Iter1&>() == std::declval<const Iter1&>()) &&
3498 noexcept(std::declval<const Iter2&>() == std::declval<const Iter2&>())) {
3499 return first_current_ == first_end_ || second_current_ == second_end_;
3500 }
3501
3502 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ZipAdapter begin() const
3503 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ZipAdapter>) {
3504 return *this;
3505 }
3506
3507 [[nodiscard]] constexpr ZipAdapter end() const
3508 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ZipAdapter>);
3509
3510private:
3511 Iter1 first_begin_;
3512 Iter1 first_current_;
3513 Iter1 first_end_;
3514 Iter2 second_begin_;
3515 Iter2 second_current_;
3516 Iter2 second_end_;
3517};
3518
3519template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
3520 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<R1>,
3521 std::ranges::iterator_t<R2>>
3522ZipAdapter(R1&, R2&)
3523 -> ZipAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<R1>, std::ranges::iterator_t<R2>>;
3524
3525template <ExternalRange R1, ExternalRange R2>
3526 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
3527 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>
3528ZipAdapter(const R1&, const R2&)
3529 -> ZipAdapter<std::ranges::iterator_t<const R1>,
3530 std::ranges::iterator_t<const R2>>;
3531
3532template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
3533 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
3534constexpr auto ZipAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::operator++() noexcept(
3535 noexcept(std::declval<Iter1&>() != std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
3536 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter1&>()) &&
3537 noexcept(std::declval<Iter2&>() != std::declval<Iter2&>()) &&
3538 noexcept(++std::declval<Iter2&>())) -> ZipAdapter& {
3539 if (first_current_ != first_end_) {
3540 ++first_current_;
3541 }
3542 if (second_current_ != second_end_) {
3543 ++second_current_;
3544 }
3545 return *this;
3546}
3547
3548template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
3549 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
3550constexpr auto ZipAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::operator++(int) noexcept(
3551 std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ZipAdapter> &&
3552 noexcept(++std::declval<ZipAdapter&>())) -> ZipAdapter {
3553 auto temp = *this;
3554 ++(*this);
3555 return temp;
3556}
3557
3558template <typename Iter1, typename Iter2>
3559 requires ZipAdapterRequirements<Iter1, Iter2>
3560constexpr auto ZipAdapter<Iter1, Iter2>::end() const
3561 noexcept(std::is_nothrow_copy_constructible_v<ZipAdapter>) -> ZipAdapter {
3562 auto result = *this;
3563 result.first_current_ = result.first_end_;
3564 result.second_current_ = result.second_end_;
3565 return result;
3566}
3567
3568/**
3569 * @brief CRTP base class providing common adapter operations.
3570 * @details Provides chaining methods (Filter, Map, Take, Skip, etc.) that can
3571 * be used by any derived adapter class. Uses CRTP pattern to return the correct
3572 * derived type.
3573 * @tparam Derived The derived adapter class
3574 */
3575template <typename Derived>
3576class FunctionalAdapterBase {
3577public:
3578 /**
3579 * @brief Chains another filter operation on top of this iterator.
3580 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3581 * @param predicate Function to filter elements
3582 * @return FilterAdapter that applies this adapter then filters
3583 */
3584 template <typename Pred>
3585 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Filter(Pred predicate) const
3586 noexcept(noexcept(FilterAdapter<Derived, Pred>(GetDerived().begin(),
3587 GetDerived().end(),
3588 std::move(predicate)))) {
3589 return FilterAdapter<Derived, Pred>(
3590 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(predicate));
3591 }
3592
3593 /**
3594 * @brief Transforms each element using the given function.
3595 * @tparam Func Transformation function type
3596 * @param transform Function to apply to each element
3597 * @return MapAdapter that transforms adapted results
3598 */
3599 template <typename Func>
3600 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Map(Func transform) const
3601 noexcept(noexcept(MapAdapter<Derived, Func>(GetDerived().begin(),
3602 GetDerived().end(),
3603 std::move(transform)))) {
3604 return MapAdapter<Derived, Func>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3605 std::move(transform));
3606 }
3607
3608 /**
3609 * @brief Limits the number of elements to at most count.
3610 * @param count Maximum number of elements to yield
3611 * @return TakeAdapter that limits adapted results
3612 */
3613 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Take(size_t count) const
3614 noexcept(noexcept(TakeAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3615 GetDerived().end(), count))) {
3616 return TakeAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3617 count);
3618 }
3619
3620 /**
3621 * @brief Skips the first count elements.
3622 * @param count Number of elements to skip
3623 * @return SkipAdapter that skips adapted results
3624 */
3625 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Skip(size_t count) const
3626 noexcept(noexcept(SkipAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3627 GetDerived().end(), count))) {
3628 return SkipAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3629 count);
3630 }
3631
3632 /**
3633 * @brief Takes elements while a predicate is true.
3634 * @tparam Pred Predicate type for take-while operation
3635 * @param predicate Predicate to determine when to stop taking
3636 * @return TakeWhileAdapter that conditionally takes elements
3637 */
3638 template <typename Pred>
3639 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto TakeWhile(Pred predicate) const
3640 noexcept(noexcept(TakeWhileAdapter<Derived, Pred>(
3641 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(predicate)))) {
3642 return TakeWhileAdapter<Derived, Pred>(
3643 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(predicate));
3644 }
3645
3646 /**
3647 * @brief Skips elements while a predicate is true.
3648 * @tparam Pred Predicate type for skip-while operation
3649 * @param predicate Predicate to determine when to stop skipping
3650 * @return SkipWhileAdapter that conditionally skips elements
3651 */
3652 template <typename Pred>
3653 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto SkipWhile(Pred predicate) const
3654 noexcept(noexcept(SkipWhileAdapter<Derived, Pred>(
3655 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(predicate)))) {
3656 return SkipWhileAdapter<Derived, Pred>(
3657 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(predicate));
3658 }
3659
3660 /**
3661 * @brief Adds an index to each element.
3662 * @return EnumerateAdapter that pairs indices with values
3663 */
3664 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Enumerate() const
3665 noexcept(noexcept(EnumerateAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3666 GetDerived().end()))) {
3667 return EnumerateAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end());
3668 }
3669
3670 /**
3671 * @brief Observes each element without modifying it.
3672 * @tparam Func Inspector function type
3673 * @param inspector Function to call on each element
3674 * @return InspectAdapter for side effects
3675 */
3676 template <typename Func>
3677 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Inspect(Func inspector) const
3678 noexcept(noexcept(InspectAdapter<Derived, Func>(GetDerived().begin(),
3679 GetDerived().end(),
3680 std::move(inspector)))) {
3681 return InspectAdapter<Derived, Func>(
3682 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), std::move(inspector));
3683 }
3684
3685 /**
3686 * @brief Takes every Nth element.
3687 * @param step Step size between elements
3688 * @return StepByAdapter that skips elements
3689 */
3690 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto StepBy(size_t step) const
3691 noexcept(noexcept(StepByAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3692 GetDerived().end(), step))) {
3693 return StepByAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3694 step);
3695 }
3696
3697 /**
3698 * @brief Chains another range after this one.
3699 * @tparam OtherIter Iterator type of the other adapter
3700 * @param begin Begin iterator of the other adapter
3701 * @param end End iterator of the other adapter
3702 * @return ChainAdapter that yields elements from both ranges
3703 */
3704 template <typename OtherIter>
3705 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Chain(OtherIter begin, OtherIter end) const
3706 noexcept(noexcept(ChainAdapter<Derived, OtherIter>(GetDerived().begin(),
3707 GetDerived().end(),
3708 std::move(begin),
3709 std::move(end)))) {
3710 return ChainAdapter<Derived, OtherIter>(GetDerived().begin(),
3711 GetDerived().end(),
3712 std::move(begin), std::move(end));
3713 }
3714
3715 /**
3716 * @brief Chains another range after this one.
3717 * @tparam R Range type of the other adapter
3718 * @param range The other range to chain
3719 * @return ChainAdapter that yields elements from both ranges
3720 */
3721 template <typename R>
3722 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Chain(R& range) const
3723 noexcept(noexcept(ChainAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>(
3724 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range))) {
3725 return ChainAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>(
3726 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range);
3727 }
3728
3729 /**
3730 * @brief Chains another range after this one.
3731 * @tparam R Range type of the other adapter
3732 * @param range The other range to chain
3733 * @return ChainAdapter that yields elements from both ranges
3734 */
3735 template <typename R>
3736 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Chain(const R& range) const
3737 noexcept(noexcept(ChainAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>(
3738 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range))) {
3739 return ChainAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>(
3740 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range);
3741 }
3742
3743 /**
3744 * @brief Reverses the order of elements.
3745 * @note Requires bidirectional iterator support
3746 * @return ReverseAdapter that yields elements in reverse order
3747 */
3748 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Reverse() const
3749 noexcept(noexcept(ReverseAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3750 GetDerived().end()))) {
3751 return ReverseAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end());
3752 }
3753
3754 /**
3755 * @brief Creates sliding windows over elements.
3756 * @param window_size Size of the sliding window
3757 * @return SlideAdapter that yields windows of elements
3758 * @warning window_size must be greater than 0
3759 */
3760 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Slide(size_t window_size) const
3761 noexcept(noexcept(SlideAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3762 GetDerived().end(),
3763 window_size))) {
3764 return SlideAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3765 window_size);
3766 }
3767
3768 /**
3769 * @brief Takes every Nth element with stride.
3770 * @param stride Number of elements to skip between yields
3771 * @return StrideAdapter that yields every Nth element
3772 * @warning stride must be greater than 0
3773 */
3774 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Stride(size_t stride) const
3775 noexcept(noexcept(StrideAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(),
3776 GetDerived().end(), stride))) {
3777 return StrideAdapter<Derived>(GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(),
3778 stride);
3779 }
3780
3781 /**
3782 * @brief Zips another range with this one.
3783 * @tparam OtherIter Iterator type to zip with
3784 * @param begin Begin iterator for the other range
3785 * @param end End iterator for the other range
3786 * @return ZipAdapter that yields tuples of corresponding elements
3787 */
3788 template <typename OtherIter>
3789 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Zip(OtherIter begin, OtherIter end) const
3790 noexcept(noexcept(ZipAdapter<Derived, OtherIter>(GetDerived().begin(),
3791 GetDerived().end(),
3792 std::move(begin),
3793 std::move(end)))) {
3794 return ZipAdapter<Derived, OtherIter>(GetDerived().begin(),
3795 GetDerived().end(), std::move(begin),
3796 std::move((end)));
3797 }
3798
3799 /**
3800 * @brief Zips another range with this one.
3801 * @tparam R Other range
3802 * @param range The other range to zip with
3803 * @return ZipAdapter that
3804 */
3805 template <typename R>
3806 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Zip(R& range) const
3807 noexcept(noexcept(ZipAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>(
3808 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range))) {
3809 return ZipAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<R>>(
3810 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range);
3811 }
3812
3813 /**
3814 * @brief Zips another range with this one.
3815 * @tparam R Other range
3816 * @param range The other range to zip with
3817 * @return ZipAdapter that
3818 */
3819 template <typename R>
3820 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Zip(const R& range) const
3821 noexcept(noexcept(ZipAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>(
3822 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range))) {
3823 return ZipAdapter<Derived, std::ranges::iterator_t<const R>>(
3824 GetDerived().begin(), GetDerived().end(), range);
3825 }
3826
3827 /**
3828 * @brief Terminal operation: applies an action to each element.
3829 * @tparam Action Function type that processes each element
3830 * @param action Function to apply to each element
3831 */
3832 template <typename Action>
3833 constexpr void ForEach(const Action& action) const;
3834
3835 /**
3836 * @brief Terminal operation: reduces elements to a single value using a
3837 * folder function.
3838 * @tparam T Accumulator type
3839 * @tparam Folder Function type that combines accumulator with each element
3840 * @param init Initial accumulator value
3841 * @param folder Function to fold elements
3842 * @return Final accumulated value
3843 */
3844 template <typename T, typename Folder>
3845 [[nodiscard]] constexpr T Fold(T init, const Folder& folder) const;
3846
3847 /**
3848 * @brief Terminal operation: finds the first element satisfying a predicate.
3849 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3850 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3851 * @return Optional or pointer to the element with maximum key, based on the
3852 * iterator type
3853 */
3854 template <typename Pred>
3855 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Find(const Pred& predicate) const;
3856
3857 /**
3858 * @brief Terminal operation: counts elements satisfying a predicate.
3859 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3860 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3861 * @return Number of elements that satisfy the predicate
3862 */
3863 template <typename Pred>
3864 [[nodiscard]] constexpr size_t CountIf(const Pred& predicate) const;
3865
3866 /**
3867 * @brief Terminal operation: partitions elements into two groups based on a
3868 * predicate.
3869 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3870 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3871 * @return Pair of vectors: first contains elements satisfying predicate,
3872 * second contains the rest
3873 */
3874 template <typename Pred>
3875 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Partition(const Pred& predicate) const;
3876
3877 /**
3878 * @brief Terminal operation: partitions elements with a custom allocator.
3879 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3880 * @tparam Allocator Allocator for result vectors
3881 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3882 * @param allocator Allocator to use for both result vectors
3883 * @return Pair of vectors using the provided allocator
3884 */
3885 template <typename Pred, typename Allocator>
3886 requires std::same_as<typename Allocator::value_type,
3887 std::iter_value_t<Derived>>
3888 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto PartitionWith(const Pred& predicate,
3889 Allocator allocator) const;
3890
3891 /**
3892 * @brief Terminal operation: finds the element with the maximum value
3893 * according to a key function.
3894 * @tparam KeyFunc Key extraction function type
3895 * @param key_func Function to extract comparison key from each element
3896 * @return Optional or pointer to the element with maximum key, based on the
3897 * iterator type
3898 */
3899 template <typename KeyFunc>
3900 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto MaxBy(const KeyFunc& key_func) const;
3901
3902 /**
3903 * @brief Terminal operation: finds the element with the minimum value
3904 * according to a key function.
3905 * @tparam KeyFunc Key extraction function type
3906 * @param key_func Function to extract comparison key from each element
3907 * @return Optional or pointer to the element with maximum key, based on the
3908 * iterator type
3909 */
3910 template <typename KeyFunc>
3911 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto MinBy(const KeyFunc& key_func) const;
3912
3913 /**
3914 * @brief Terminal operation: groups elements by a key function.
3915 * @tparam KeyFunc Key extraction function type
3916 * @param key_func Function to extract grouping key from each element
3917 * @return Map from keys to vectors of elements with that key
3918 */
3919 template <typename KeyFunc>
3920 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto GroupBy(const KeyFunc& key_func) const;
3921
3922 /**
3923 * @brief Terminal operation: groups elements by key with custom allocators.
3924 * @tparam KeyFunc Key extraction function type
3925 * @tparam MapAllocator Allocator for unordered_map nodes
3926 * @tparam ValueAllocator Allocator for grouped vectors
3927 * @param key_func Function to extract grouping key from each element
3928 * @param map_allocator Allocator for map storage
3929 * @param value_allocator Allocator for each grouped vector
3930 * @return Map from keys to vectors using provided allocators
3931 */
3932 template <typename KeyFunc, typename MapAllocator, typename ValueAllocator>
3933 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto GroupByWith(
3934 const KeyFunc& key_func, MapAllocator map_allocator,
3935 ValueAllocator value_allocator) const;
3936
3937 /**
3938 * @brief Terminal operation: collects all elements into a vector.
3939 * @return Vector containing all elements
3940 */
3941 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto Collect() const;
3942
3943 /**
3944 * @brief Terminal operation: collects all elements into a vector with a
3945 * custom allocator.
3946 * @tparam Allocator Allocator type
3947 * @param allocator Allocator to use for the vector
3948 * @return Vector containing all elements
3949 */
3950 template <typename Allocator>
3951 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto CollectWith(Allocator allocator = {}) const;
3952
3953 /**
3954 * @brief Terminal operation: collects all elements into a vector with a
3955 * specific memory resource.
3956 * @param resource Memory resource to use for the vector
3957 * @return Vector containing all elements
3958 */
3959 [[nodiscard]] constexpr auto CollectWith(
3960 std::pmr::memory_resource* resource) const;
3961
3962 auto CollectWith(std::nullptr_t) const = delete;
3963
3964 /**
3965 * @brief Terminal operation: writes all elements into an output iterator.
3966 * @details Consumes the adapter and writes each element to the provided
3967 * output iterator. This is more efficient than `Collect()` when you already
3968 * have a destination container.
3969 * @tparam OutIt Output iterator type
3970 * @param out Output iterator to write elements into
3971 *
3972 * @code
3973 * std::vector<int> results;
3974 * query.Filter([](int x) { return x > 5;
3975 * }).Into(std::back_inserter(results));
3976 * @endcode
3977 */
3978 template <typename OutIt>
3979 constexpr void Into(OutIt out) const;
3980
3981 /**
3982 * @brief Terminal operation: checks if any element satisfies a predicate.
3983 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3984 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3985 * @return True if any element satisfies the predicate, false otherwise
3986 */
3987 template <typename Pred>
3988 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool Any(const Pred& predicate) const;
3989
3990 /**
3991 * @brief Terminal operation: checks if all elements satisfy a predicate.
3992 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
3993 * @param predicate Function to test elements
3994 * @return True if all elements satisfy the predicate, false otherwise
3995 */
3996 template <typename Pred>
3997 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool All(const Pred& predicate) const;
3998
3999 /**
4000 * @brief Terminal operation: checks if no elements satisfy a predicate.
4001 * @tparam Pred Predicate type
4002 * @param predicate Function to test elements
4003 * @return True if no elements satisfy the predicate, false otherwise
4004 */
4005 template <typename Pred>
4006 [[nodiscard]] constexpr bool None(const Pred& predicate) const {
4007 return !Any(predicate);
4008 }
4009
4010protected:
4011 /**
4012 * @brief Gets reference to derived class instance.
4013 * @return Reference to derived class
4014 */
4015 [[nodiscard]] constexpr Derived& GetDerived() noexcept {
4016 return static_cast<Derived&>(*this);
4017 }
4018
4019 /**
4020 * @brief Gets const reference to derived class instance.
4021 * @return Const reference to derived class
4022 */
4023 [[nodiscard]] constexpr const Derived& GetDerived() const noexcept {
4024 return static_cast<const Derived&>(*this);
4025 }
4026};
4027
4028template <typename Derived>
4029template <typename Action>
4030constexpr void FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::ForEach(
4031 const Action& action) const {
4032 for (auto&& value : GetDerived()) {
4033 if constexpr (std::invocable<Action, decltype(value)>) {
4034 action(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4035 } else {
4036 std::apply(action, std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4037 }
4038 }
4039}
4040
4041template <typename Derived>
4042template <typename T, typename Folder>
4043constexpr T FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::Fold(T init,
4044 const Folder& folder) const {
4045 for (auto&& value : GetDerived()) {
4046 if constexpr (std::invocable<Folder, T&&, decltype(value)>) {
4047 init = folder(std::move(init), std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4048 } else {
4049 init = std::apply(
4050 [&init, &folder](auto&&... args) {
4051 return folder(std::move(init),
4052 std::forward<decltype(args)>(args)...);
4053 },
4054 std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4055 }
4056 }
4057 return init;
4058}
4059
4060template <typename Derived>
4061template <typename Pred>
4062constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::Find(
4063 const Pred& predicate) const {
4064 using IterType = decltype(GetDerived().begin());
4065 using Result = details::find_result_t<IterType>;
4066
4067 for (auto&& value : GetDerived()) {
4068 bool result = false;
4069 if constexpr (std::invocable<Pred, decltype(value)>) {
4070 result = predicate(value);
4071 } else {
4072 result = std::apply(predicate, value);
4073 }
4074 if (result) {
4075 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4076 return Result{&value};
4077 } else {
4078 return Result{value};
4079 }
4080 }
4081 }
4082
4083 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4084 return Result{nullptr};
4085 } else {
4086 return Result{std::nullopt};
4087 }
4088}
4089
4090template <typename Derived>
4091template <typename Pred>
4092constexpr size_t FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::CountIf(
4093 const Pred& predicate) const {
4094 size_t count = 0;
4095 for (const auto& value : GetDerived()) {
4096 bool result = false;
4097 if constexpr (std::invocable<Pred, decltype(value)>) {
4098 result = predicate(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4099 } else {
4100 result = std::apply(predicate, std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4101 }
4102 if (result) {
4103 ++count;
4104 }
4105 }
4106 return count;
4107}
4108
4109template <typename Derived>
4110template <typename Pred>
4111constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::Partition(
4112 const Pred& predicate) const {
4113 using ValueType = std::iter_value_t<Derived>;
4114 std::vector<ValueType> matched;
4115 std::vector<ValueType> not_matched;
4116
4117 for (auto&& value : GetDerived()) {
4118 bool result = false;
4119 if constexpr (std::invocable<Pred, decltype(value)>) {
4120 result = predicate(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4121 } else {
4122 result = std::apply(predicate, std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4123 }
4124
4125 if (result) {
4126 matched.push_back(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4127 } else {
4128 not_matched.push_back(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4129 }
4130 }
4131
4132 return std::pair{std::move(matched), std::move(not_matched)};
4133}
4134
4135template <typename Derived>
4136template <typename Pred, typename Allocator>
4137 requires std::same_as<typename Allocator::value_type,
4138 std::iter_value_t<Derived>>
4139constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::PartitionWith(
4140 const Pred& predicate, Allocator allocator) const {
4141 using ValueType = std::iter_value_t<Derived>;
4142 std::vector<ValueType, Allocator> matched(allocator);
4143 std::vector<ValueType, Allocator> not_matched(std::move(allocator));
4144
4145 for (auto&& value : GetDerived()) {
4146 bool result = false;
4147 if constexpr (std::invocable<Pred, decltype(value)>) {
4148 result = predicate(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4149 } else {
4150 result = std::apply(predicate, std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4151 }
4152
4153 if (result) {
4154 matched.push_back(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4155 } else {
4156 not_matched.push_back(std::forward<decltype(value)>(value));
4157 }
4158 }
4159
4160 return std::pair{std::move(matched), std::move(not_matched)};
4161}
4162
4163template <typename Derived>
4164template <typename KeyFunc>
4165constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::MaxBy(
4166 const KeyFunc& key_func) const {
4167 using IterType = decltype(GetDerived().begin());
4168 using Result = details::find_result_t<IterType>;
4169
4170 auto iter = GetDerived().begin();
4171 const auto end_iter = GetDerived().end();
4172
4173 if (iter == end_iter) {
4174 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4175 return Result{nullptr};
4176 } else {
4177 return Result{std::nullopt};
4178 }
4179 }
4180
4181 auto get_key = [&key_func](auto&& val) {
4182 if constexpr (std::invocable<KeyFunc, decltype(val)>) {
4183 return key_func(std::forward<decltype(val)>(val));
4184 } else {
4185 return std::apply(key_func, std::forward<decltype(val)>(val));
4186 }
4187 };
4188
4189 Result max_element;
4190 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4191 max_element = &(*iter);
4192 } else {
4193 max_element.emplace(*iter);
4194 }
4195
4196 auto max_key = get_key(*max_element);
4197 ++iter;
4198
4199 while (iter != end_iter) {
4200 auto current_key = get_key(*iter);
4201 if (current_key > max_key) {
4202 max_key = std::move(current_key);
4203 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4204 max_element = &(*iter);
4205 } else {
4206 max_element.emplace(*iter);
4207 }
4208 }
4209 ++iter;
4210 }
4211 return max_element;
4212}
4213
4214template <typename Derived>
4215template <typename KeyFunc>
4216constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::MinBy(
4217 const KeyFunc& key_func) const {
4218 using IterType = decltype(GetDerived().begin());
4219 using Result = details::find_result_t<IterType>;
4220
4221 auto iter = GetDerived().begin();
4222 const auto end_iter = GetDerived().end();
4223
4224 if (iter == end_iter) {
4225 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4226 return Result{nullptr};
4227 } else {
4228 return Result{std::nullopt};
4229 }
4230 }
4231
4232 auto get_key = [&key_func](auto&& val) {
4233 if constexpr (std::invocable<KeyFunc, decltype(val)>) {
4234 return key_func(std::forward<decltype(val)>(val));
4235 } else {
4236 return std::apply(key_func, std::forward<decltype(val)>(val));
4237 }
4238 };
4239
4240 Result min_element;
4241 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4242 min_element = &(*iter);
4243 } else {
4244 min_element.emplace(*iter);
4245 }
4246
4247 auto min_key = get_key(*min_element);
4248 ++iter;
4249
4250 while (iter != end_iter) {
4251 auto current_key = get_key(*iter);
4252 if (current_key < min_key) {
4253 min_key = std::move(current_key);
4254 if constexpr (details::IterYieldsReference<IterType>) {
4255 min_element = &(*iter);
4256 } else {
4257 min_element.emplace(*iter);
4258 }
4259 }
4260 ++iter;
4261 }
4262
4263 return min_element;
4264}
4265
4266template <typename Derived>
4267template <typename KeyFunc>
4268constexpr auto FunctionalAdapterBase<Derived>::GroupBy(
4269 const KeyFunc& key_func) const {
